source_title: Distal Tibia Allograft Workstation for Glenoid Bone Loss
text: Glenoid Drill Guide Handle, long AR-9215-1-02 Please note that not all products advertised in this brochure / surgical technique guide may be available in all countries. Please ask Arthrex Customer Service or your local Arthrex Representative before ordering if the desired product is available for delivery. Distal Tibia Allograft Workstation for Glenoid Bone Loss I 11 References 1. Provencher MT, Ghodadra N, LeClere L, Solomon DJ, Romeo AA. Anatomic osteochondral glenoid reconstruction for recurrent glenohumeral instability with glenoid deficiency using a distal tibia allograft. Arthroscopy . 2009;25(4):446-452. doi:10.1016/j.arthro.2008.10.017 2. Provencher MT, Frank RM, Golijanin P, et al. Distal tibia allograft glenoid reconstruction in recurrent anterior shoulder instability: clinical and radiographic outcomes. Arthroscopy . 2017;33(5):891-897. doi:10. 1016/j.arthro.2016.09.029 This description of technique is provided as an educational tool and clinical aid to assist properly licensed medical professionals in the usage of specific Arthrex products. As part of this professional usage, the medical professional must use their professional judgment in making any final determinations in product usage and technique. In doing so, the medical professional should rely on their own training and experience and should conduct a thorough review of pertinent medical literature and the product’s directions for use. Postoperative management is patient-specific and dependent on the treating professional’s assessment. Individual results will vary and not all patients will experience the same postoperative activity level or outcomes.
